summary.intent: Implement application-level HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy support for moltis so users behind corporate firewalls can route all outbound traffic through a configured proxy.
summary.outcome: Added `upstream_proxy` config field to `moltis.toml` with full propagation to providers, tools, OAuth, MCP auth, Slack, MS Teams, and web_fetch. Telegram proxy support was deferred due to teloxide's reqwest 0.11 version mismatch. All 1285 tests pass and clippy is clean.

repo: Two shared HTTP clients exist: `moltis_tools::shared_http_client()` (had proxy init but only for sandbox) and `moltis_providers::shared_http_client()` (used `Client::new()` with no proxy at all).
~30+ places in the codebase create `reqwest::Client::new()` independently across channels, voice providers, OAuth, memory embeddings, and MCP auth.
The trusted-network sandbox proxy is a local domain-allowlist filter (feature-gated), separate from the user-facing upstream proxy — both can be active simultaneously.
Workspace uses Rust 2024 edition with `unsafe_code = 'deny'` at workspace level, which blocks `std::env::set_var` usage.
teloxide-core depends on reqwest 0.11 with `default-features = false`, making it impossible to pass a reqwest 0.12 `Proxy` type to `Bot::with_client()`.
`build_http_client` utility was moved to `moltis-common` so both `moltis-tools` and `moltis-providers` can share it without circular dependencies.
A `OnceLock<String>` global accessor pattern (`set_upstream_proxy` / `upstream_proxy_url`) in `moltis-common` allows channels to read the proxy URL without threading it through every plugin API.
CUDA build failures on macOS when running `--all-features` is a pre-existing issue; macOS lint/test recipes explicitly exclude CUDA-dependent crates.
taplo reformats TOML feature lists alphabetically on `fmt` — sorting `reqwest` features is expected behavior, not a bug.

code.finding: New module housing `build_http_client(proxy_url: Option<&str>)` and global `upstream_proxy_url()` / `set_upstream_proxy()` accessors via OnceLock.
WebFetchTool has its own `proxy_url` field for the sandbox proxy; updated to fall back to `upstream_proxy_url()` when sandbox proxy is not set.
Reverted all proxy changes — teloxide uses reqwest 0.11 internally; cannot pass reqwest 0.12 Proxy type; relies on env var fallback instead.
Upstream proxy is initialized before the trusted-network block; a `#[cfg(not(feature = 'trusted-network'))]` block handles tools client init when sandbox feature is off.
Added `upstream_proxy` to `build_schema_map()` top-level struct and semantic URL validation in `check_semantic_warnings()`.

workflow: Running `cargo clippy` without `--all-features` is required on macOS to avoid CUDA build failures; use feature-specific flags for providers/gateway.
When adding reqwest dependencies to new crates, also add the `socks` feature to the workspace Cargo.toml for SOCKS5 proxy support.
Check `Cargo.toml` of target crates for `moltis-common` dependency before using shared utilities — several crates already had it, some (like `moltis-oauth`, `moltis-mcp`) needed it added.

friction: teloxide's internal reqwest 0.11 dependency made it impossible to apply proxy settings to the Telegram bot — multiple approaches were attempted (unsafe set_var, wrapping ClientBuilder, using client_from_env) before settling on a documented limitation.
Rust 2024 edition's unsafe `set_var` restriction (combined with workspace-level `unsafe_code = 'deny'`) blocked the env-var-at-startup approach for Telegram proxy.
`--all-features` clippy/test runs fail on macOS due to CUDA/llama-cpp build requirements — required manual feature exclusion for clean CI runs.
The `or_else` lifetime issue in `web_fetch.rs` when chaining `proxy_url` fallback required multiple iterations to resolve.
open_items: Telegram proxy support is deferred until teloxide upgrades to reqwest 0.12 — currently only `HTTPS_PROXY` env var works for Telegram.
Per-channel proxy configuration (different proxy per channel) was explicitly deferred as a follow-up; current implementation is application-level only.
Voice providers were listed in the plan but not explicitly updated in the transcript — may still use `Client::new()` directly.
Memory embeddings client was listed in the plan but not explicitly updated — may still use `Client::new()` directly.
Integration test for proxy config end-to-end (actual proxied HTTP request) was mentioned in the plan but not implemented.
